title: Infrastructure-as-code drift — applied state diverges from merged plan
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate the validity of the merged plan itself.
evidence: Saved search 'iac_drift' (every 30 min) compares cloud inventory against the last-merged IaC plan checksum and lists divergences.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CM-6 configuration settings require that the applied state match the approved baseline.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST-800-53 CM-6 (Configuration settings) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.42.5: Infrastructure-as-code drift — applied state diverges from merged plan.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.42.5' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SOC-2 CC8.1 (Change management) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.42.5: Infrastructure-as-code drift — applied state diverges from merged plan.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.42.5' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: A.8.9 configuration management requires monitoring of drift from baselines.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that ISO-27001 A.8.9 (Configuration management (2022 new)) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.42.5: Infrastructure-as-code drift — applied state diverges from merged plan.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A manual console change alters a security-group rule that was established via Terraform; the next drift scan finds the difference.
controlTest.negativeScenario: All applied changes carry the current plan's checksum; no alert.

dataSources: Terraform/OpenTofu state snapshots, cloud provider inventory (AWS Config, Azure Resource Graph, GCP Asset Inventory), CI/CD merge events.
spl:
index=iac sourcetype=tf:state_snapshot earliest=-30m
| stats latest(plan_checksum) as expected_checksum by resource_id
| join resource_id [search index=cloud sourcetype=aws:config OR sourcetype=azure:rg OR sourcetype=gcp:asset earliest=-30m | stats latest(state_checksum) as actual_checksum by resource_id]
| where expected_checksum!=actual_checksum
| table resource_id expected_checksum actual_checksum
description: Continuously reconciles the cloud resource inventory against the last-approved IaC plan checksum and alerts on drift. Drift is a canonical change-management deficiency and also a known attacker-persistence pattern.
value: Turns 'we use Terraform' into a continuously-verified statement.
implementation: (1) Export per-plan checksums from CI on merge; (2) enable cloud inventory export; (3) run every 30 min; (4) SOAR auto-remediates via `terraform plan && apply` if allowed. Indexes required: `index=cloud`, `index=iac`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=aws:config`, `sourcetype=azure:rg`, `sourcetype=gcp:asset`, `sourcetype=tf:state_snapshot`.
visualization: Heatmap of resources × drift, table of drifting resources, single-value '% of resources matching plan'. Key fields: `actual_checksum`, `expected_checksum`, `resource_id`, `resource_id]`.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1562.001 Impair Defenses: Disable Tools

knownFalsePositives: Baseline drift during scheduled patches, controlled config updates, or planned upgrades.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Terraform/OpenTofu state snapshots, cloud provider inventory (AWS Config, Azure Resource Graph, GCP Asset Inventory), CI/CD merge events..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Export per-plan checksums from CI on merge; (2) enable cloud inventory export; (3) run every 30 min; (4) SOAR auto-remediates via `terraform plan && apply` if allowed.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=iac sourcetype=tf:state_snapshot earliest=-30m
| stats latest(plan_checksum) as expected_checksum by resource_id
| join resource_id [search index=cloud sourcetype=aws:config OR sourcetype=azure:rg OR sourcetype=gcp:asset earliest=-30m | stats latest(state_checksum) as actual_checksum by resource_id]
| where expected_checksum!=actual_checksum
| table resource_id expected_checksum actual_checksum
```

Understanding this SPL

**Infrastructure-as-code drift — applied state diverges from merged plan** — Turns 'we use Terraform' into a continuously-verified statement.

Documented **Data sources**: Terraform/OpenTofu state snapshots, cloud provider inventory (AWS Config, Azure Resource Graph, GCP Asset Inventory), CI/CD merge events. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: iac; **sourcetype**: tf:state_snapshot. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=iac, sourcetype=tf:state_snapshot, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **resource_id**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where expected_checksum!=actual_checksum` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: actual_checksum, expected_checksum, resource_id, resource_id].

Step 3 — Validate
Compare results with the audit register and the GRC system so evidence lines up with control owners, attestations, and retention policy.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap of resources × drift, table of drifting resources, single-value '% of resources matching plan'.
grandmaExplanation: We help you see when configs drift from what you baselined, so only approved change moves through.
